import json
import re

# Load the business data from var_functions.query_db:14
business_data = [{"business_id": "businessid_49", "review_count": "8", "description": "Located at 6901 Phelps Rd in Goleta, CA, this facility offers a nurturing environment for young learners, providing a range of services in Education, Elementary Schools, Child Care & Day Care, Local Services, Preschools, and Montessori Schools."}, {"business_id": "businessid_47", "review_count": "81", "description": "Located at 9916 Clayton Rd in St. Louis, MO, this establishment offers a wide range of services, including Hair Salons, Beauty & Spas, Hair Stylists, Skin Care, Blow Dry/Out Services, and Makeup Artists."}, {"business_id": "businessid_88", "review_count": "39", "description": "Located at 11655 W Executive Dr in Boise, ID, this facility offers enthusiasts a premier destination for Gun/Rifle Ranges, Active Life."}, {"business_id": "businessid_41", "review_count": "5", "description": "Located at 1615 Pasadena Ave S, Ste 430 in Saint Petersburg, FL, this facility offers a range of services in Internal Medicine, Doctors, Health & Medical."}, {"business_id": "businessid_33", "review_count": "28", "description": "Located at 9655 E US Hwy 36, Unit H in Avon, IN, this establishment offers a range of services including Nail Salons, Hair Removal, Beauty & Spas, and Waxing."}]

# Extract state information from descriptions
state_reviews = {}
state_business_ids = {}

for business in business_data:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    review_count = int(business['review_count'])
    description = business['description']
    
    # Extract state using regex - look for pattern like "City, ST"
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        if state not in state_reviews:
            state_reviews[state] = 0
            state_business_ids[state] = []
        state_reviews[state] += review_count
        state_business_ids[state].append(business_id)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state_reviews': state_reviews,
    'state_business_ids': state_business_ids
}))

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. args.code:
import json
import re

# Load business data
business_file = var_functions.query_db:28
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

state_reviews = {}
state_business_ids = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state from description
    if description:
        match = re.search(r'in [^,]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})', description)
        if match:
            state = match.group(1)
            if state not in state_reviews:
                state_reviews[state] = 0
                state_business_ids[state] = []
            state_reviews[state] += review_count
            state_business_ids[state].append(business_id)

# Find state with highest reviews
max_state = max(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state': max_state[0],
    'total_reviews': max_state[1],
    'business_ids': state_business_ids[max_state[0]]
}))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load business data from the file path stored in the variable
business_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:28']
with open(business_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

state_reviews = {}
state_business_ids = {}

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    review_count = int(business.get('review_count', 0))
    description = business.get('description', '')
    
    # Extract state from description
    if description:
        match = re.search(r'in [^,]+,\s*([A-Z]{2})', description)
        if match:
            state = match.group(1)
            if state not in state_reviews:
                state_reviews[state] = 0
                state_business_ids[state] = []
            state_reviews[state] += review_count
            state_business_ids[state].append(business_id)

# Find state with highest reviews
max_state = max(state_reviews.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state': max_state[0],
    'total_reviews': max_state[1],
    'business_ids': state_business_ids[max_state[0]]
}))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json

# Load the review ratings for Missouri businesses
review_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:36']
with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

# Extract all ratings and calculate average
ratings = [int(review['rating']) for review in reviews]
average_rating = sum(ratings) / len(ratings) if ratings else 0

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'state': 'MO',
    'total_reviews': 2243,
    'average_rating': round(average_rating, 2),
    'number_of_reviews_analyzed': len(ratings)
}))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
